steps.title: Use the equal radii
steps.content: Because $C$ is the center and $M$ and $N$ are on the circle, $CM$ and $CN$ are radii of the same circle. Therefore, triangle $CMN$ is isosceles, so the two base angles are equal.

steps.title: Split the remaining angle
steps.content: The angle at $C$ is $84^\circ$, so the other two angles total $$ 180^\circ-84^\circ=96^\circ $$ Since the two base angles are equal, each is $$ \frac{96^\circ}{2}=48^\circ $$ The answer is 48.
